title: Create architecture documentation for Tracks spoke engineering onboarding
perceive: Spoke has excellent external documentation (README, prompts, samples) but internal architecture documentation is minimal. A new engineer joining this spoke lacks onboarding docs for lug schema, advisor roles, and framework integration.
execute: 1. Create ARCHITECTURE.md (or expand AGENTS.md) with sections:
- Spoke Identity: What Tracks is, what it is not, deliverables, non-deliverables
- Directory Structure: Explain prompts/, spec/, samples/, WAI-Spoke/, Skills/, .claude/ and purpose of each
- Lug Schema: Explain lug fields used in this spoke (type, status, routed_to, initiative values specific to Tracks)
- Advisor System: Explain each advisor (documentation, framework_development) and their roles
- Signal Routing: How signals are processed, routed to hub, integration points
- Teaching Adoption: How teachings flow from hub, adoption workflow, wai-context.md updates
- Work Lifecycle: Diagram of lug state (open → in_progress → completed), signal delivery
2. Include diagrams (ASCII or link to visual) showing spoke lifecycle.
3. Link to KnowMe.md and CLAUDE.md as reference points.
verify: ARCHITECTURE.md exists, covers all sections above, is internally consistent, links to other docs work, suitable for engineer onboarding.
acceptance_criteria: File created, comprehensive for new engineer, 300-500 word range, no broken references, linked from README.
execution_notes: ARCHITECTURE.md created with all required sections: spoke identity, directory structure, lug schema, advisor system, signal routing, teaching adoption, work lifecycle diagrams, and ASCII flow charts. File linked from README.md. All sections suitable for engineer onboarding. Acceptance criteria met: file exists, covers all sections, internally consistent, all internal links verified to work.
